Hmm, so if my rankings are all around the same percentile, maybe I'm not optimizing my working hours effectively. Maybe the peak times have more demand but also more competition. If everyone is working during the same hours, it's harder to stand out.

I should consider adjusting my schedule to work during less busy periods when there might be fewer riders competing for orders. That could mean starting earlier or finishing later than the typical shift. 

If I start earlier, say at 7:00 AM, I can capture the morning rush before others arrive. Alternatively, ending later, like 19:00 (7 PM), might let me catch evening deliveries when other riders have already stopped.

I need to balance the potential increase in orders with my own stamina and efficiency. Starting earlier might allow me to work fewer hours but earn more if the demand is higher during those times. On the other hand, working later could extend my earning window without overlapping too much with peak rider times.

Considering all this, maybe I should adjust my start time slightly earlier or end a bit later to avoid the busiest periods and maximize my earnings.

- Riding distance: I was ranked 33rd out of 100. That's not bad; it means about 67% of riders rode more than me. So I'm somewhere in the middle to lower half.
- Money earned: I came in 48th, which is right around average. Slightly below if we consider 50 as the median.
- Number of orders received: 38th place. Again, better than average but still not top tier.

Hmm, so my earnings are about average, but I delivered more orders and covered less distance compared to others. That might mean that either I took longer routes or perhaps didn't take as many high-paying orders.

Wait, if I rode less distance but got fewer orders, does that mean my efficiency isn't great? Maybe I'm taking too long per order, which could be why my earnings are only average despite handling a decent number of deliveries.

I should think about how to improve. One way is to optimize the time I spend on each delivery. Maybe by choosing routes more efficiently or picking up orders that have higher pay and shorter distances.

But looking at the hours I worked yesterday, from 9 AM to 9 PM, that's a full day without breaks. Perhaps this isn't sustainable, especially as a new rider adjusting to the job. It might be causing me to burn out or make mistakes due to fatigue.

So maybe I should adjust my working hours today. Instead of starting at 9 AM right away, perhaps start later when there's less rush, and end earlier to avoid the late-night traffic which can be stressful and time-consuming.

Let me consider the peak times. Usually, breakfast orders pick up around 7-10 AM, lunch from 12-2 PM, and dinner from 6-9 PM. By starting at 10 AM, I might miss some of the early morning rush but could catch the lunch surge better. Also, finishing at 8 PM would allow me to cover the dinner peak without staying too late when traffic is heavy or riders are more exhausted.

Starting later and ending earlier could help me focus my efforts during high-demand periods when orders are more frequent and perhaps pay better. This might increase the number of orders I complete and boost my earnings, potentially moving me up in the rankings.

I should also consider how much time I need to rest between shifts. Working 10 hours today instead of 12 might help with stamina and focus, leading to fewer mistakes and more efficient deliveries.

So, planning to start at 10 AM and finish at 8 PM seems like a better balance. It gives me enough time during peak hours without overworking, which could improve both the number of orders and my earnings.
